2605.15100.title: Dual-Dimensional Consistency: Balancing Budget and Quality in Adaptive Inference-Time Scaling
2605.15100.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated remarkable abilities in reasoning. However, maximizing their potential through inference-time scaling faces challenges in trade-off between sampling budget and reasoning quality. Current strategies remain inefficient as they typically treat sampling width and depth as orthogonal objectives, where width consensus methods risk reinforcing hallucinations, while depth pruning mechanisms prematurely truncate complex yet valid reasoning chains. Therefore, we propose Dual-Dimensional Consistency (DDC), a unified framework that bridges path quality with adaptive termination. By coupling Confidence-Weighted Bayesian protocol with a Trend-Aware Stratified Pruning, our method ensures that computational resources are concentrated on high quality reasoning paths, filtering hallucinations while accelerating consensus. Evaluations across five benchmarks demonstrate that this approach reduces token consumption by over 10 times while maintaining or exceeding the accuracy of strong baselines across various LLMs.
2605.15100.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在推理方面展現了卓越的能力。<lb/>然而，通過推理時間的擴展來最大化其潛力面臨著在取樣預算和推理質量之間的權衡挑戰。<lb/>目前的策略仍然效率不高，因為它們通常將取樣的寬度和深度視為正交目標，其中寬度共識方法有可能加強幻覺，而深度修剪機制則過早地截斷複雜但有效的推理鏈。<lb/>因此，我們提出了雙維一致性（DDC），這是一個統一框架，將路徑質量與自適應終止相連接。<lb/>通過將信心加權貝葉斯協議與趨勢感知分層修剪相結合，我們的方法確保計算資源集中於高質量的推理路徑，過濾幻覺的同時加速共識。<lb/>在五個基準測試中的評估表明，這種方法在保持或超越各種LLMs的強基準準確性的同時，將標記消耗降低了超過10倍。
2605.15100.author: Rongman Xu et.al.
2605.15100.authors: Rongman Xu, Yifei Li, Tianzhe Zhao, Yanrui Wu, Bo Li, Hang Yan
